name: Maya
communication_level: verbal, 3-4 word phrases, can read sight words
interests: Jurassic World raptors (specifically Blue the raptor)

iep_goals.description: Maya will initiate or respond to peer greetings (verbal or gesture-based) with 80% accuracy across 10 trials in a variety of settings (classroom arrival, lunch, specials, free play) as measured by teacher observation tally.

trial_history.notes: Peer buddy program just starting; shy during group transition
More responsive to familiar peers; still avoids larger groups
Fire drill caused sensory overload; but recovered well in 1:1 interactions
Morning routine established; consistent greeting responses in familiar context
Met target! Consistent across multiple settings; reinforcer stickers helping
Peer greeting tally directly measures communication goal
Peer greeting tally directly measures communication goal

iep_goals.description: Maya will follow two-step verbal directions (without visual supports) with 75% accuracy across 8 trials during independent work time, classroom routines, and transitions, as measured by teacher checklist.

trial_history.notes: Relies on visual schedule; struggles with verbal-only two-step directions
Down from baseline; distracted by new peer buddy; needs refocus
Back to baseline; added verbal cueing helping (e.g., 'listen with your whole body')
Improvement with routine directions; still needs support for novel two-step tasks
Met target! Positive trend when directions paired with visual checklist as prompt
Math accuracy and engagement indicate direction-following progress
Math accuracy and engagement indicate direction-following progress

iep_goals.description: Maya will reduce the frequency and duration of outbursts (tantrums, crying, verbal aggression) to an average of 1 or fewer per school day, with duration under 5 minutes, monitored across 5-week intervals using behavior log tracking.

trial_history.notes: 2.4 outbursts/day average; loud fluorescent lights in art room triggering
2.6 outbursts/day; transition to recess still difficult
1.8 outbursts/day; fire drill = 2 major meltdowns but weighted vest helped calm
1.2 outbursts/day average; noise-canceling headphones during transitions working well
1 outburst this week (down from 6 last week); fidget cube + weighted lap pad routine established
